answer:
We compute the curl of \(\mathbf{F}\):

\[
\nabla \times \mathbf{F} = 
\begin{vmatrix}
\mathbf{i} & \mathbf{j} & \mathbf{k} \\
\partial_x & \partial_y & \partial_z \\
xy & yz & zx
\end{vmatrix}
= (-y)\mathbf{i} + (-z)\mathbf{j} + (-x)\mathbf{k}.
\]

The triangle lies in the plane \(x+y+z=1\). Using Stokes’ Theorem, the surface integral of the curl over the triangle (oriented upward) equals the line integral. Parameterizing the surface as \(z=1-x-y\) with \(0\le x,\; y,\; x+y\le 1\), the upward normal is \((1,1,1)\). Then

\[
(\nabla \times \mathbf{F})\cdot \mathbf{n} = -y - z - x = -(x+y+z) = -1.
\]
